reasoning: The ON RRP award rate is an administered rate set by the Federal Reserve, which exactly tracks the bottom of the Federal Funds target range (currently set at 3.5% after the January 27-28, 2026 FOMC meeting). Since it is not market-determined, the only way it changes is via an official policy adjustment. According to the stated 2026 schedule, the FOMC's next meeting is on March 17-18, 2026. Any change to the policy rate announced at that meeting would not become effective until March 19 at the earliest. Therefore, on both February 22 and March 17, the rate will mechanically remain exactly 3.5% unless there is an unprecedented inter-meeting emergency hike. Given the current macroeconomic context where the Fed has been cutting rates and the market expects further cuts or pauses (dropping to ~3% by year-end), the probability of an inter-meeting hike is exceptionally near zero. To account for structural minimum probabilities and slightly increasing uncertainty for the latter date, estimates of 5% and 6% have been set.
